applied_optimizations.name: Triton fused bias_sub_tanh kernel
applied_optimizations.description: Implemented fused Triton kernel for bias subtraction and tanh activation. Used manual tanh implementation (exp-based) since tl.math.tanh is unavailable on ROCm. BLOCK_SIZE=1024 aligned with AMD wavefront size (64).

notes: tl.math.tanh is unavailable on ROCm Triton - must use manual implementation: tanh(x) = (exp(2x) - 1) / (exp(2x) + 1)
BLOCK_SIZE should be multiples of 64 (AMD wavefront size) for optimal performance
The ConvTranspose2d operation dominates at 65.6% - this is MIOpen's implementation and hard to optimize without custom conv kernels
The internal bias add of ConvTranspose2d (16.8%) is a separate kernel launch from MIOpen - not fusible at application level
Our Triton kernel fuses the explicit bias subtraction with tanh, achieving 1.146x speedup over pure PyTorch
Further optimization would require either: (1) custom ConvTranspose2d kernel, or (2) finding a way to fuse the internal bias add
